name: Vendure Headless Commerce API
what_it_does: Open-source TypeScript/Node.js headless ecommerce framework with GraphQL API-first architecture. Designed for developers building custom commerce experiences without monolithic CMS constraints.
use_cases: Building headless ecommerce storefronts with any frontend framework via GraphQL
Automating product catalog, inventory, and pricing management via Admin API
Creating custom fulfillment workflows and order management automation
Extending commerce logic with custom plugins and resolvers
Multi-channel commerce (web, mobile, POS) from a single GraphQL backend
not_for: Non-technical merchants needing a hosted SaaS solution
Simple stores where Shopify simplicity is preferred over flexibility
best_when: A developer team wants full control over ecommerce logic via TypeScript plugins and GraphQL, without being locked into a SaaS platform.
avoid_when: You need a managed SaaS ecommerce with built-in hosting and non-developer-friendly admin.

auth.notes: Admin API uses session-based auth or API keys. Storefront API uses customer sessions. Channel-based permissions for multi-tenant setups. Custom auth guards can be implemented via plugins.

pricing.notes: Vendure framework is MIT open-source. Vendure Cloud offers managed hosting. Strong TypeScript ecosystem and active community. Plugin marketplace available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL schema is self-documenting. GraphQL Playground/Altair available for exploration. Error responses follow GraphQL conventions with structured error extensions. TypeScript types provide excellent IDE support.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL mutations are not inherently idempotent. Product/variant creation can be made idempotent using external reference codes. Order state transitions are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL only — no REST API; agents need GraphQL client library
Admin and Storefront APIs are separate GraphQL schemas with different auth
Custom plugins required for business logic extensions — not configuration-driven
Job queue system for async operations must be monitored separately

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open-source with TypeScript type safety. Channel-based access control. Admin API behind authentication. Node.js security depends on deployment configuration. Active security maintenance.
